programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve heads into the heart of the Suriname Jungle. He goes in search of poison dart frogs, deadly snakes and hopes to find the elusive giant river otter.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve goes in search of the deadliest cats in Africa in the carnivore capital of Zambia. He gets up close to the rulers of the Savannah - the lions and leopards.

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: Alan and the final five designers head to Leeds to transform studio rental apartments. They must cleverly divide open-plan spaces and inject character into show apartments to entice potential renters. Former Interior Design Masters winner Lynsey Ford joins Michelle to critique the designs, and they are blown away by the dramatically different results.

programme.title: Green Book
programme.desc: New York, 1962: Italian-American nightclub bouncer Tony Lip gets taken on as a driver for acclaimed African American pianist Donald Shirley on a tour of the Deep South, where racism and segregation are still commonplace.

programme.title: Kidnapped by My Mum
programme.desc: At 11 years old, Alex vanished on holiday with his mother and grandfather for six years. Now, he's retracing his steps across Europe to tell the full story for the first time.

programme.title: Confessions of a Teenage Fraudster
programme.desc: A cautionary tale about one of the UK’s most notorious credit card fraudsters, whose five-year crime wave began at just 16 years of age. While his contemporaries were using fake IDs to get into nightclubs, he was defrauding innocent cardholders from his desk in a call centre, laying the groundwork for a journey that would open up a world of wealth, first-class travel and an escape from the life he seemed destined for. But, as he soon discovers, this lifestyle would be laced with darkness, danger and terrible consequences.

programme.title: Children of the Blitz
programme.desc: From September 1940 to May 1941, during the Second World War, over 35,000 tonnes of bombs and incendiaries were dropped on towns and cities across the UK during the infamous Luftwaffe aerial bombing campaign that became known as the Blitz. Just before the outbreak of war, the British government’s Operation Pied Piper put plans in place to evacuate millions of children, but only 800,000 left towns and cities that were vulnerable to attack. These are the stories of some of the two million children who stayed at home, often to help their families or because their parents couldn’t bear to send them away. This documentary features some of the last people who witnessed the reality of the Blitz, with their testimony offering an intimate, enlightening and moving portrait of wartime Britain through children’s eyes.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Great Central Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo’s railway adventure through Uzbekistan reaches its finale in the historic city of Bukhara. Amidst the medieval Islamic architecture, Michael goes in search of a centuries-old Jewish community. Stepping inside a 420-year-old synagogue and a family home, he’s introduced to unique customs and traditions, and a fragrant green dish that has made Bukharan Jews famous worldwide. Michael grapples with kurash, an ancient form of upright wrestling once used to select the bravest warriors and now also increasingly popular as a sport for young Uzbek women. Michael’s exploration of Uzbekistan culminates in the vast Kyzylkum Desert, where he experiences the life of nomadic tribes who once roamed its vast sandy plains. His Central Asian journey concludes among the horses and riders who brave all to take part in one of the most dangerous equestrian sports in the world.

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein lead the search, with guest judge and passionate gardener Penny Lancaster, to find Northern Ireland’s greatest garden. The judges face a difficult decision when it comes to choosing. Ivydene garden near Comber is spread across three acres. Owners Robert and Michael have three separate areas of planting - a formal layout at the front of their home, a secret garden at the side, and at the back, a wildflower meadow and woodland area. In total contrast, there’s a tropical backyard over in Lisburn, where owner George has sourced unusual plants from all around the world. Finally, the judges view a rock garden in Ballynahinch. A large expanse of native rock dominates this four-acre garden, created by passionate owner Brigid. Diarmuid, Carol and Penny are searching for a garden which the owners have poured their heart and soul into creating. Which will they decide is worthy of a place in the Greatest Garden final?

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: In Brighton, Zoe Ball wants to help retired woodworker Neville get his health back on track after a series of heart attacks. Her prescription is delightfully simple: Neville should add a little sunshine into his life. Just ten minutes of sunlight a day has been shown to improve heart health, strengthen bones and lift your mood. A small dose of daylight could make a big difference.

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents highlights from seven games, including Newcastle against West Ham, on the penultimate weekend of the Premier League season, with Nuno Espirito Santo’s side desperate for points in the relegation battle. Aston Villa take on Liverpool, Nottingham Forest go to Manchester United, and there is plenty to play for in the race for the final few European spots as Brentford host Crystal Palace, Brighton make the trip to Leeds, Everton face Sunderland, and Wolves welcome Fulham to Molineux.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The big political issues of the day.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards presents the quiz with a third dimension. In traditional quiz shows, questions have just right and wrong answers. But here, there is a twist - the Impossible answer. It's not just wrong, it couldn't possibly be right. Each day, 24 regular contestants compete for a possible £10,000 jackpot. Right answers keep them on track for the big money, but if at any stage they give an impossible answer, that immediately knocks them out, and they must try again the next day. Every show climaxes with the £10,000 question, as the daily winner faces one last challenge. Three right answers will trigger a spectacular cascade of 10,000 pound coins from a giant exclamation mark, but a single impossible answer means they will leave with nothing.

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Four more contenders take to the black chair under the spotlight and the ticking clock to answer questions on their specialist subjects and general knowledge. Clive Myrie asks the questions, which are on Offa's Dyke Path, the life and works of John Cage, the wines of Portugal and Only Fools and Horses.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio. The contestants’ motivation in each round is simple: stay out of the light and you’ll be all right!

programme.title: Clean It, Fix It
programme.desc: The team visit north London to help solve a family of four's housing woes. In one day, and on a family friendly budget, they have to create a home office for a busy working mum, deep clean the appliances and find space for the family’s plant collection.

programme.title: Wonders of Life
programme.desc: In the world's most volcanic region, south east Asia, Prof Brian Cox explores the thin line that separates the living from the dead, posing the enduring question: what is life?

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel invites members of the public to bring along their antiques for examination. The team travel to St Austell in Cornwall where they encounter some unusual objects, including a sock-making machine and two stuffed eagles. They also find personal memorabilia from a member of the 1932 Olympic swimming team, a gold watch given for a heroic exploit and a lifetime's collection of greetings cards.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: This episode comes from Oxford Union, right in the heart of central Oxford. Paul Martin is joined by experts Christina Trevanion and Will Axon. Together the team pick out a selection of interesting antiques and collectables to be sold at a local auction. Will uncovers some salacious etchings by a very famous artist, while Christina finds a unique autograph book, but will it reach for the stars or languish at the bottom of the bill? Paul also gets the chance to explore behind the scenes at Oxford Union to discover a few of the famous faces who debated in the chamber.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Josh Jones, Molly Rainford, Mark Rhodes and Sonia take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games wheelie luggage, or will they be tempted with the very popular bee hotel?

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on a stunning railway adventure through the western Mediterranean islands of Sardinia and Corsica, beginning in Sardinia’s capital, Cagliari. Michael delves into the city's Roman past and experiences warm Sardinian hospitality with a taste of the traditional myrtle liqueur. He discovers the legacy of Eleanora de Arborea, the pioneering medieval ruler who shaped Sardinia’s laws, and listens to the unique sounds of the Sardinian language, Sardo, and its haunting choral singing. Travelling west to Porto Flavia, he marvels at an extraordinary feat of engineering, an underground railway and tunnel which revolutionised the transport of the island’s prized minerals to the Mediterranean.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores some of this year's most exciting archaeological finds from the north of Britain. Each discovery comes straight from the site, filmed by the archaeologists themselves. Alice discovers the well-preserved writing tablets, swords and domestic items left by Romans at Vindolanda during a time of British rebellion. On the Scottish island of Iona, there are traces of a long-lost monastery and pilgrimage site that was originally built by the legendary Saint Columba, and has been compared to Jerusalem. In the east of Scotland, a weapons hoard belonging to a wealthy Bronze Age warrior is unearthed.

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Monty Don and Rachel de Thame are joined by a team of gardening experts, including Carol Klein, Adam Frost and Jamie Butterworth. They explore some of the most innovative and inspiring gardens, and discover the beautiful floral exhibits in the Great Pavilion. Also, Sophie Raworth welcomes the royal family to the showground.

programme.title: Lucy Worsley Investigates
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley investigates one of history’s most dramatic break-ups: when, in 1776, America declared its independence from Great Britain, sparking the American Revolution. This eight-year war claimed thousands of lives and ended in a humiliating defeat for King George III. Lucy sets out to uncover why America chose this path and how Britain’s missteps fractured an empire and shook the monarchy, and asks whether this explosive split could have been avoided.

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob head to the beautiful River Ouse in the heart of Bedfordshire. The river was once famous for barbel, but has recently become renowned for its big perch instead, a fish that has so far eluded them. After a long day fishing under the summer sun on the Turvey estate, Bob prepares a dinner that Paul will never forget - slow-cooked tripe.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Victoria Derbyshire.

programme.title: This Is a Bomb: The Nevada Casino Heist
programme.desc: When the bomb detonates inside Harvey’s Wagon Wheel Casino, it leaves behind more than shattered concrete and twisted steel. There's a maze of unanswered questions, until a lucky break leads to a psychological stand-off. Calculated risks unravel, loyalties facture, and the truth proves far more complicated than anyone imagined.

programme.title: Destroyer
programme.desc: Damaged LAPD detective Erin Bell embarks on a last-ditch personal mission to track down charismatic criminal Silas, whom she went undercover to expose years earlier. That operation imploded, and Bell’s personal failings could sabotage this new quest, as they have already destroyed her family life. Starring Nicole Kidman

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Charlotte Smith and Adam Henson visit Oswestry Livestock Market, one of the region’s largest weekly cattle and sheep markets. As spring trading peaks, they step into a fast-moving world where fortunes can change in minutes. With livestock numbers falling and markets closing nationwide, Oswestry remains a crucial lifeline, not just for trade, but for farming communities to come together.

programme.title: Twenty Twenty Six
programme.desc: With the start of the tournament weeks away, final plans for the opening ceremony need to be pinned down. But its Mexican creative director has had a rethink, and his new vision doesn’t include any reference to America at all, or indeed to soccer. The lead events producer may or may not be close to a having a breakdown. Can Ian and the oversight team find a solution in time? Meanwhile, Ian finds himself starting to think about a future beyond Twenty Twenty Six and Miami. Will Sarah be part of that future?

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Denise Nurse heads to Carmarthenshire for a heartwarming homecoming as a couple return to familiar territory with their young daughter. Currently based in the busy Berkshire town of Wokingham, the Paralympic gold medallist rower and his physiotherapist wife have £400,000 to find their perfect family home. Along the way, Denise also catches up with another growing family who have already made their own escape to this beautiful part of the country.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Roo Irvine takes to the streets to quiz the good folk of Leominster in Herefordshire about her mystery object. Encased in brass with a screw top at one end, what is it? Across town, two teams are puzzling over what to buy with their budgets of £300. Guiding the red team is Charles Hanson. Friends Toni and Debbie reckon they know how to get their way when it comes to bagging a bargain. Let’s see if they can out-haggle the blue team, friends and company bosses Russ and Jay. With antiques expert Richard Madley by their side, the blues tune into a Bakelite radio – but are they on the same wavelength when it comes to the price? Meanwhile, the reds consider pouring their money into a rustic jug – until they see the price tag. Can they exterminate the competition with a vintage Dalek toy instead? With their shopping bags full, the teams cross the border into north Wales to sell their items at Matthew Tong’s auction. Which team is his money on?

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on a stunning railway adventure through the western Mediterranean islands of Sardinia and Corsica, beginning in Sardinia’s capital, Cagliari. Michael delves into the city's Roman past and experiences warm Sardinian hospitality with a taste of the traditional myrtle liqueur. He discovers the legacy of Eleanora de Arborea, the pioneering medieval ruler who shaped Sardinia’s laws, and listens to the unique sounds of the Sardinian language, Sardo, and its haunting choral singing. Travelling west to Porto Flavia, he marvels at an extraordinary feat of engineering, an underground railway and tunnel which revolutionised the transport of the island’s prized minerals to the Mediterranean.

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.desc: Across the south of England, farms are being targeted by a ruthless criminal gang, stealing vehicles, machinery and power tools worth tens of thousands of pounds. With just a single number plate to go on, a determined detective in Hertfordshire begins painstakingly piecing together a pattern of thefts that crosses county lines. As the investigation rapidly unfolds, his hunch leads to a major breakthrough, and the dramatic capture of two men behind a £44,000 crime spree. In Leicestershire, a specialist fire and rescue team join forces with a local farmer to rescue a heavily pregnant cow that has found herself trapped in a swimming pool. And in south Wales, reporter Paul Colgan meets the scientists pioneering an extraordinary new forensic technique that uses soil DNA to track illegal off-road bikers.

programme.title: Lost and Found in the Lakes
programme.desc: The detectorists battle the elements to find a long-lost ring on the fells, thrown from the hand of a shepherd while tending his sheep 60 years ago. Social media detective Mui takes on a phone found by an underwater drone, which leads her on her first international search for an owner. Meanwhile, wood crafter Stef showcases some of the finest traditional lake district artefacts from our discarded finds.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The big political issues of the day.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards presents the quiz with a third dimension. In traditional quiz shows, questions have just right and wrong answers. But here, there is a twist - the Impossible answer. It's not just wrong, it couldn't possibly be right. Each day, 24 regular contestants compete for a possible £10,000 jackpot. Right answers keep them on track for the big money, but if at any stage they give an Impossible answer, that immediately knocks them out, and they must try again the next day. Every show climaxes with the £10,000 question as the daily winner faces one last challenge. Three right answers trigger a spectacular cascade of 10,000 pound coins from a giant exclamation mark, but a single Impossible answer means they will leave with nothing.

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie hosts as the series continues with another four contenders taking to the black chair, answering questions on their chosen specialist subjects to earn a place in the semi-finals. The specialist subjects are Neil Armstrong, Shakespeare's comedies, the Jacobite Rebellion of 1745 and Rupert Bear.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio. The contestants’ motivation in each round is simple: stay out of the light and you’ll be all right!

programme.title: Clean It, Fix It
programme.desc: Single mum Hayley and her daughter Rosie Bell live in a former council house with virtually no storage space. The team help them to reclaim some empty wall space and find a way to upcycle a piece of furniture that gives them a place to eat together.

programme.title: Wonders of Life
programme.desc: Brian Cox travels across the USA offering a unique retelling of evolutionary history, encountering some astonishing animals that reveal how the senses evolved and how they operate.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel invites members of the public to bring along their antiques for examination. Interesting finds include a finger exerciser, a costume worn by Yul Brynner, a poacher's gun disguised as a walking stick and a collection of Oxo memorabilia. There are valuable items too - a collection of 100 silver teething rattles, a small pottery cat bought for £2 and worth £5,000, and a rare Anglo-Saxon ring raked up with the hedge clippings.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: In this edition, Flog It! flies around the UK, touching down at historic hotspots like Capesthorne Hall in Cheshire, where Anita Manning has a surprise in store for one lucky lady. Paul Martin visits Chedworth Roman Villa in the Cotswolds.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Josh Jones, Molly Rainford, Mark Rhodes and Sonia take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games wheelie luggage, or will they be tempted with the very popular bee hotel?

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo’s railway tour of Sardinia heads inland towards the island's dramatic mountain landscapes, which he discovers were frequented by DH Lawrence, who is revered in this remote spot. Michael visits the spectacular Nuraghe, mysterious Bronze Age conical stone structures unique to Sardinia, where remarkable artefacts have been unearthed to reveal an advanced society of engineers, navigators and craftsmen. Immersing himself in Sardinian traditions, Michael visits a family-run agriturismo, where he learns the art of making delicate ravioli. Finally, he embarks on a nostalgic journey aboard Il Trenino Verde, one of Europe’s longest narrow-gauge railways, which winds through Sardinia’s rugged interior.

programme.title: Marcus Wareing Simply Provence
programme.desc: Marcus loves a British fruit-based pudding and is searching Provence for the local equivalent. After trying out a prawn cocktail served in a half-melon on local farmers, he changes tack and meets with a local chef for a France v Britain pudding cook-off. He’s also looking for the tastiest local cheese to take to the village pétanque game. He discovers a Provençal favourite is goat's cheese, and so he visits a local producer who gives him a whirlwind apprenticeship in all things goat.

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: It’s Medals Day at Chelsea, and emotions are running high as the all-important medals are handed out. Monty Don and Rachel de Thame showcase the standout gardens across the showground, while Carol Klein reveals what it takes to grow gold medal-winning plants in the Great Pavilion. Adam Frost has inspirational design ideas for your garden at home, and Jamie Butterworth explores some of the new plants on display. And the winner of the RHS Chelsea Garden of the Year is unveiled.

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig is in search of victory with Rob Beckett, Daliso Chaponda, Lara Ricote and Alan Davies.

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve and a team of world-class kayakers head to the Kamchatka Peninsula in the far east of Russia to take on the first descent of the Kronotsky River – through white water so extreme that it has never before been attempted. The team set out to paddle from source to sea, starting in the shadow of a still-active volcano and ending at the Pacific Ocean – kayaking through an unspoilt wilderness packed with more brown bears than almost anywhere else on the planet. If the team can make it through the white water, they will enter an untouched landscape and witness brown bears in their natural habitat, unaffected by humans. These Russian brown bears are so isolated, some of them may never have seen people before.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Victoria Derbyshire.

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: The world's most famous football show, with the big names and the big games from the Premier League.

programme.title: Later... with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Jools welcomes pop sensation Niall Horan from County Westmeath, Ireland, who makes his debut on the show and performs a couple of songs, including the title track from his fourth album Dinner Party. London legends Squeeze make a welcome return to music with their first album since 2017, a collection of stories set in a fictional nightclub called Trixies. They perform a couple of tracks and chat to Jools about how the record came about. Also joining the line-up is dance-pop duo TOMORA, who are Norwegian singer-songwriter Aurora and Tom Rowlands, one half of The Chemical Brothers. They have combined their talents and names to create a wildly inventive band and perform a track from their debut album COME CLOSER. Californian surrealist blues poet, lyricist and activist aja monet performs her track elsewhere from her second album the color of rain, described as 'an album born from experimental jazz that plays with rhymes and rhythms in a way that gives them a psychedelic quality'. Completing the line-up is Bristolian duo Getdown Services, known for their explosive live performances. They perform The Radiator, a song about 'only picking fights that you know you’ll win'.

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: In the penultimate leg, the teams enter Mongolia, the final country of the race, battling freezing conditions and brutal off-road travel to reach Kharkhorin.

programme.title: Iolo's River Valleys
programme.desc: Iolo Williams explores the Tywi river valley, from Llansteffan’s coastal dunes to fallow deer ruts, ancient oak woodlands and the remote heights of the Tywi forest.

programme.title: This Is a Bomb: The Nevada Casino Heist
programme.desc: Meticulous and methodical, Big John engineers his ransom scheme with military precision. On paper, it’s flawless. But as the deadline approaches, nothing goes according to plan, and two battles unfold simultaneously: a high-stakes ransom exchange and a desperate race to stop a bomb designed to be unstoppable. Precision planning meets a chaotic reality, and one miscalculation on either side could mean catastrophe.

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: Surgeons face a difficult dilemma in the middle of a life-saving bile duct cancer operation. A chest sarcoma proves more challenging than expected, with no room for error.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Richie Anderson is house-hunting in North Yorkshire with Sarah. who is on the hunt for a beautiful stone cottage in a thriving community. With a budget of £220,000 and support from her son Christian, they explore a range of tempting, characterful homes. Richie also visits a young couple who have embraced rural life on their farm, where they’ve started what they claim is the world’s first egg restaurant.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Let’s hope it’s not all downhill for two teams of bargain hunters as they scour the stalls of an antiques centre in Kingston upon Thames.

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo’s railway tour of Sardinia heads inland towards the island's dramatic mountain landscapes, which he discovers were frequented by DH Lawrence, who is revered in this remote spot. Michael visits the spectacular Nuraghe, mysterious Bronze Age conical stone structures unique to Sardinia, where remarkable artefacts have been unearthed to reveal an advanced society of engineers, navigators and craftsmen. Immersing himself in Sardinian traditions, Michael visits a family-run agriturismo, where he learns the art of making delicate ravioli. Finally, he embarks on a nostalgic journey aboard Il Trenino Verde, one of Europe’s longest narrow-gauge railways, which winds through Sardinia’s rugged interior.

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael travels to the cultural heart of Japan, Kyoto, the city that for a thousand years was the capital. At the Rozanji temple, he learns about a book that is described as the world’s first novel, the Tale of Genji. Across the city, Michael visits the last remaining wagasa umbrella workshop in Kyoto. At Kyoto’s Matsunoo shrine, Michael gets a beginner’s guide to shrine etiquette and discovers the countless daily Shinto rituals that have become part of Japanese life. The day ends with Michael taking a trip to a local restaurant to meet Satoha, an apprentice geisha, to find out how this ancient artform is adapting to modern life.

programme.title: Great Korean Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael’s South Korean railway journey continues in Seoul. Travelling across one of the world’s largest metro systems, he heads south to Suwon to visit Samsung’s headquarters, tracing the rise of the family-run company from a small trading business to a global technology giant and symbol of Korea’s postwar economic miracle. Touring a futuristic smart home, Michael explores how innovation now shapes everyday life. Back in central Seoul, he visits the city’s original railway station, built during Japanese rule and now preserved as a cultural space, reflecting the complexities of Korea’s colonial past. In Gangnam, a district propelled to global stardom by a K-pop hit, he explores the international success of Korean popular culture, meeting the boss and dancers at a K-pop academy to understand how Hallyu – the Korean cultural wave – has conquered international audiences. Finally, in Seodaemun, Michael visits a former prison and Independence Park to uncover the story of the March First Movement of 1919, a pivotal moment in Korea’s struggle for independence from Japan, meeting a descendant of a leading resistance figure whose courage helped shape Korea’s struggle for freedom.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The big political issues of the day.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards presents the quiz with a third dimension. In traditional quiz shows, questions have just right and wrong answers. But here, there is a twist - the Impossible answer. It's not just wrong, it couldn't possibly be right. Each day, 24 regular contestants compete for a possible £10,000 jackpot. Right answers keep them on track for the big money, but if at any stage they give an Impossible answer, that immediately knocks them out and they must try again the next day. Every show climaxes with the £10,000 question as the daily winner faces one last challenge. Three right answers trigger a spectacular cascade of 10,000 pound coins from a giant exclamation mark, but a single Impossible answer means they will leave with nothing.

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: The hunt for the nation's Mastermind continues as Clive Myrie tests four more contenders on their specialist subjects and general knowledge. The specialist subjects are the Peter Wimsey stories of Dorothy L Sayers, Arnold Schwarzenegger, The Wire and the Falklands conflict.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio. The contestants’ motivation in each round is simple: stay out of the light and you’ll be all right!

programme.title: Clean It, Fix It
programme.desc: The Olgun family's rented two-bed flat in south west London is bursting at the seams. Their hallway is a pinch point with outdated storage and clutter in full view, so the team create a bespoke worktop and revamp the tired units to make the space fit for purpose. Then the much-used kitchen and bathroom are deep cleaned, ridding them of grease and limescale.

programme.title: Wonders of Life
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox asks how, from a lifeless cosmos ruled by the laws of physics and chemistry, it is possible that a planet can produce so much wonderful, varied biology.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: For the last programme in the present series Michael Aspel and the experts visit Harrogate in Yorkshire. Among the items they unearth are naval log books belonging to a Captain who escorted Napoleon to St Helena, 'one of the most lovely silk bedcovers' Bunny Campione has ever seen and an 'absolutely gorgeous' carriage clock made for the daughter of a great Victorian philanthropist.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Members of the public try to make money out of their antiques.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Josh Jones, Molly Rainford, Mark Rhodes and Sonia take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games wheelie luggage, or will they be tempted with the very popular bee hotel?

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his Sardinian railway adventure, arriving in Mamoiada to investigate the town’s extraordinary pagan traditions. In a menacing mask, black sheepskin and 30 kg of cowbells, Michael embraces the role of a mamuthone, who wards off evil spirits. Taking a 1950s diesel railcar, Michael journeys across the island to Macomer, where he discovers the railway’s surprising connection to a pioneering Welsh railway engineer. His adventure concludes at Italy’s largest cork factory, where a team of expert sniffers safeguard the integrity of the world’s finest wines.

programme.title: Iolo's River Valleys
programme.desc: Iolo explores the Teifi river valley and finds a slow worm hunting, meets a coracle fisherman, is thrilled by a fishing otter and watches a pair of kestrels at their nest.

programme.title: The Future with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: Can artificial intelligence read emotions better than a human? Hannah explores the new technology that is creeping into our lives.

programme.title: The Secret Genius of Modern Life
programme.desc: The rollercoaster has been sending our hearts racing and stomachs dropping for over a century, and today, millions of us get our kicks on these topsy-turvy, daredevil joyriders. Hannah gets the inside story on the UK’s newest, tallest and fastest rollercoaster – Thorpe Park’s Hyperia. She uncovers the wild origins of the modern-day coaster and finds out how some dodgy French physics in the 19th century, Nasa rocket scientists and an ambitious cartoonist called Walt delivered the ultimate thrill machine.

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Monty Don and Arit Anderson explore how gardening can inspire us to be more creative while celebrating the artistry on display in the showground and Great Pavilion. Rachel de Thame reveals who has been named RHS Florist of the Year 2026, and Adam Frost delves into the latest design trends and explains how you can replicate them at home.

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: A surgeon must remove part of his patient’s tongue and reconstruct it to save her from an aggressive cancer, and surgeons perform a rare simultaneous pancreas and kidney transplant.

programme.title: Peelers: The PSNI for Real
programme.desc: Stephen joins response officers Anna and Perri as they attend a 999 call made from a property in Belfast. A man has had bleach squirted directly on his face and into his eyes during a domestic dispute. At risk to their own safety, the officers enter the property to find the suspect upstairs, reportedly self-harming. There are reports he has strangled a dog. Across town, Tom and Nigel race to the scene of a serious crash that has left a pedestrian badly injured. As soon as they arrive, a scared and tearful woman approaches. She thinks the man lying in the middle of the road, being worked on by paramedics, is her partner. It’s down to Tom to deliver a message the woman will never forget. Later, adrenaline levels spike as Stephen and the team race to reports of an angry crowd surrounding the home of a convicted sex offender. As the tension rises, Stephen engages with the increasingly irate crowd, while Nigel formulates a plan to get the sex offender out of his house to safety.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Victoria Derbyshire.

programme.title: Unspun World with John Simpson
programme.desc: Unspun World provides an unvarnished version of the week's major global news stories - reliable, honest and essential viewing. The BBC's world affairs editor, John Simpson, brings his experience and expertise to bear on the most important international stories. In discussion with the BBC's unparalleled range of experts across the world, he uses their unique local knowledge to go beyond the headlines to get to the unspun truth about the world.

programme.title: The Turning
programme.desc: Supernatural thriller based on a story by Henry James. A young woman becomes the governess of two unruly children at a secluded manor.

programme.title: This is CBBC!
programme.desc: Tune every day from 7.00am.

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: Renovations force Bitzer to camp out for the night. When the flock are shut out of the barn, they must also pitch up in the field. The sheep are loving their trendy glampsite at first, but as the weather worsens and batteries run low, the appeal of Bitzer’s practical tent grows.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Animated series. When Penfold scores a date with superstar Scarlett Johamster, Danger Mouse and the Professor provide conflicting advice.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: The forest has run out of bath sponges and is becoming very stinky. Their latest order never arrived, and so the heroes must quest under the sea to Merhaven to collect them. However, Hoofius, whose singing stinks worse than his armpits, decides to enter the annual Merhaven Song Contest and accidentally causes a simple quest to collect bath sponges to turn into something much more perilous.

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: The kids find a treasure map, causing treasure-hunting fever to descend on the family. It doesn't help that Cheese is livestreaming the whole thing, broadcasting every move.

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Topical news magazine for children.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: A helicopter takes off from the beach, leaving behind installations for a TV game.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: Plankton hasn't tried to steal the formula in a while...

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck is in charge of a campsite and has to make sure everyone follows the rules. However, Frog turns up and breaks them all - he lets goats loose and has a midnight party in his tent.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: When Duck accidentally gets a job as the band conductor at the local park, Frog is keen to play trumpet in the band - but Duck is even more keen to stop him!

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: The Odd Squad investigates strange events. Oona meets Dr O's replacement.

programme.title: Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed!
programme.desc: Dennis and his mates rebel against an outbreak of unfair fun-killing rules that are being imposed on Beanotown.

programme.title: Bitesize Daily: 9-11 Year Olds
programme.desc: Learn about longitude and latitude, locating a place, and comparing time zones.

programme.title: One Zoo Three
programme.desc: Looking to help protect the planet, Cam, Tyler and Aaron take inspiration from the zoo’s animal eco-heroes, including pygmy marmosets, Amur tigers and orangutans.

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: From terrifying Spartan mums to hidden Victorian mums, via Queen Victoria and Cleopatra, Horrible Histories looks at some of history’s most monstrous mothers.

programme.title: Blow Your Mind
programme.desc: Dr Chris and Dr Xand van Tulleken bring you the top experts on the planet to show you more things that will blow your mind! The scientists and explorers spend their last day at the berg. The science team try to work out how a mighty two-billion-tonne mega-berg melts and eventually disappears, explorer Andy Torbet creates an amazing and beautiful undersea spectacle and there's more bear trouble as the team head back to the iceberg.

programme.title: Art Ninja
programme.desc: Gavin can't find the right animal costume to run the local marathon. Ricky helps him decide by making a piranha fish bag and a baboon chalk picture inspired by artist Georgia O'Keeffe, but it's only when they go to the safari park to make art that inspiration comes from an unlikely source!

programme.title: Hey You What If?
programme.desc: Clouds might look like cotton wool, but if they really were, things would get very messy.

programme.title: Deadly Mission Shark
programme.desc: As the team prepare for their great hammerhead experience, Steve has one more dive he wants some of them to experience. He takes four of the divers out to the wreck of an old ship which, over the years, has been colonised by a myriad of marine life. The young divers enter an underwater world inhabited by stunning fish and ocean turtles. Then it's back to dry land to meet the others and join in Bimini’s annual Junkanoo festival.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: When Dr Chris realises that the horrendous cheesy smell in the lab is coming from Dr Xand’s feet, the race is on to find out why they are so pongy. Is it the feet, the sweat, the trainers?! Turns out it isn’t as simple as you might think! Elsewhere, Dr Dan and Dr Raf square up in Body Battles as they put their quad muscles to the test by trying to squat to lay an egg in a nest. Thankfully, Gladiator Dynamite is there to stop anyone cracking up and make sure that the loser gets in the ice bath. As we count down our Top 5 Silliest Named Body Parts, you can check if you have Greek feet and discover where you can find your ginglymus. In Doctors on Duty, Dr Xand goes to hospital with Maddox, who has an appointment at the cystic fibrosis clinic at the Royal Brompton. Elsewhere, Victoria has slipped and fallen on her arm at school, so she is at the emergency department in Edinburgh, while Sorin has cut his face after colliding with his classmate’s cat ear hairband!

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: Fresh from reaching the final of Britain’s Got Talent, inspirational young comedian Eva Abley has set herself the challenge of putting on a fundraising ball to raise money for her local hospital. She has less than two months to come up with a new comedy routine and attract a host of big names to support her on the night, from Lost Voice Guy to Ant and Dec. Born with cerebral palsy, Eva never knows how she will feel each day, so it's a big ask for her get it all together in time.

programme.title: Stage Stars
programme.desc: It's the start of a new school year, and the school is buzzing as boarders find out who they are sharing a dorm with and day pupils reunite at the lockers. No time is wasted on day one as the students go head-to-head for roles in the new show: Anything Goes. And this year could be life-changing for dancer Jakob as he starts his preparations for one of the world’s most prestigious international ballet competitions.

programme.title: The Football Academy
programme.desc: Meet the Tottenham Hotspur academy players. Lia Lewis picks her Three of the Best, and Dominic Solanke takes on 11-year-old Konan in Skill Showdown.

programme.title: Octonauts: Above & Beyond
programme.desc: Pearl’s glacier discovery triggers a pika rescue, an algae feast and a water stoppage.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: The shadow of a monster causes panic among the Piris, apart from Nugget, who is keen to investigate a new mystery. She finally tracks down the creature, but is all exactly as it seems?

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck has a job punting tourists along the waterways, but a bored Frog thinks he can do a better job. Thanks to Frog's interference, the pair wind up going on a wild trip down the rapids.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: Mr Friendlyface’s hat has been stolen by a Big Ole Worm, and the heroes must quest to get it back. As they trail the worm through the Prickly Desert, Trevor accidentally spikes himself all over on a cactus. But all is not lost – now that he is covered in sharp spikes, Trevor realises that he has a unique superpower, and when the time comes to confront the Big Ole Worm, it’s Spiky Trevor to the rescue!

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall heads to Timor Leste in search of the biggest animal to have ever lived - the blue whale. He also hopes to find the saltwater crocodile, one of the giants of land.

programme.title: Stage Stars
programme.desc: Senior dancer Jakob is hoping to compete in the world’s most prestigious ballet competition, but he has suffered an injury that could shatter his dreams. Meanwhile, the pressure is on for the Anything Goes cast, as rehearsals are hotting up and showtime is fast approaching – but there is always time for a classroom takeover!

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: Children's animation. Set on a remote rural farm, Shaun's flock lives a peaceful life under the watchful eye of their sheepdog Bitzer. Shaun finds a camera which some ramblers have dropped and sets about taking snapshots of the flock. Bitzer is soon on the trail of the missing camera.

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: Children's animation. A circus sets up in a nearby field, and a spellbound Timmy wanders off to investigate. Can Shaun rescue him before the farmer notices he's gone?

programme.title: Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed!
programme.desc: Miss Mistry arranges to tag along with Dennis and the gang after school, in an attempt to enhance her teaching skills and prevent school governor Pfooflepfeffer from banning her beloved teaching magazine.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: Twinkle uses her excellent pocket beast card skills when on a quest to rescue the missing Council of Happiness from Grackle the Giant.

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: One day, the kids answer the door to find Wedgie, Cheese's missing piece! Wedgie is happy to have finally found her family, but she is so cute that Cheese gets jealous. Things only get worse when Wedgie starts accusing Cheese of being mean to her.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy finds a crystal skull which gives him the power to sow discord within a group.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings discover a box filled with fortune cookies close to the Great Wall of China.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: When Duck and Frog have to change a lighthouse’s light bulb, they quickly become marooned on the island. With no food, and surrounded by water, they must try to find a way to survive.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: When Duck gets a job in a cinema projection room, Frog manages to accidentally break the projector. Can Frog save the day and entertain the audience himself?

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: SpongeBob vows to clean an entire building.

programme.title: Submarine Jim
programme.desc: Jim tries to be Captain Nice, but the Urchins take advantage.

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: Omni and Onom must track down a mysterious creature that has a very peculiar habit.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The team think Raven's supervillain father is the coolest.

programme.title: Pokémon Horizons
programme.desc: Liko, Roy and Ult reunite with Dot and Murdock in Cortondo.

programme.title: Miraculous: Tales of Ladybug & Cat Noir
programme.desc: What if someone decided to trigger an akumatization?

programme.title: The Lady Grace Mysteries
programme.desc: Grace finally thinks she has worked out the identity of the traitor - Spiderweb! But as she heads off for an epic showdown, her hidden enemy has a whole other plan.

programme.title: The Next Step
programme.desc: A talented group of teenage dancers battle for glory at the Regional Championships.

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Shini takes on racing driver Billy Monger in a subterranean go-karting challenge!

programme.title: This is CBBC!
programme.desc: Tune every day from 7.00am.

programme.title: This is CBBC!
programme.desc: Tune every day from 7.00am.

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: With the Farmer and Bitzer out at the Golden Tractor Awards, the flock take the chance to enjoy all the mod cons of the house – until they overload the power, plunging the house into total darkness. When the Farmer returns, the flock must escape the house without getting caught.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Penfold is assisting Danger Mouse, testing the security system at the British Cheese Museum. Everything is going swimmingly until a temporal glitch strikes and the pair discover the museum's prized pink parmesan has somehow vanished, with a baffled Danger Mouse left cradling the crumbs! There's barely enough time for DM to question what is going on before Danger Hedgehog and Danger Mole arrive on the scene to arrest him.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: Only wood elves have ever defeated the mighty Eye of Vernon hat, so when the Riddlemaster wears the eye to take over the forest, the heroes quest to Wood Elf Wood to get help. However, Twinkle really doesn’t want to, as wood elves are so stuck up! Struggling to contain her irritation with their high-and-mighty ways, Twinkle still manages to offend them, and the heroes wonder how they will ever save the forest now.

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: The kids audition for a movie that's shooting in town, and a reluctant Cat lands the role as lead alien. However, Cat would rather just go to the circus.

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Topical news magazine for children.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy finds a conch decorated with Maori symbols that can control whales.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: Plankton goes to jail, and the secret formula is taken as evidence!

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck and Frog must move a famous pop star’s possessions from one mansion to another. However, they are unable to do it with anything remaining in one piece.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck is working overnight in a museum on his own. To keep him company, Duck makes a Frankenstein Frog, which soon runs rampage. It’s up to Frog to save the day!

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: The Odd Squad investigates strange events. The agents go to Negative Town to track a villain.

programme.title: Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed!
programme.desc: Dennis and Gnasher accidentally do good deeds for the people of Beanotown. Intrigued by the new sensation of being thanked and appreciated, they go on a 'helping rampage', leaving a wake of mayhem behind them!

programme.title: Bitesize Daily: 9-11 Year Olds
programme.desc: Learn about trade around the world, depending on other countries, and fair trade.

programme.title: One Zoo Three
programme.desc: Meet the zoo’s powerful animals as Sonika the Asiatic lioness gets a grip in a tug-of-war and Tyler takes on Marion the sulcata tortoise in a contest of strength.

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: If you watch one song contest this year, then make sure it’s the Eurovision Song Contest! But if you watch two, then look no further than this Rat-o-vision special, where Rattus dons his sparkly, golden waistcoat for a glittering affair that brings you a host of historical bangers from around the world as our international acts battle it out to win the glittering Rat-o-vision trophy. OK, ‘glittering’ might be a strong word. Anyway, along the way, we see Thomas Edison rock out for America, Poland’s entry try to achieve world peace as well as a win, a bunch of Renaissance men sing an Italian power ballad, German nun Hildegard von Bingen seeking to inspire everyone, Greece’s entry aim for the stars, Elizabeth I somehow representing both England and Spain, and three Egyptian pharaohs sing a song from beyond the grave! Who will win the coveted* trophy? Tune in to find out... *coveted by Rattus – we’re not sure about anyone else.

programme.title: Go Get Arty
programme.desc: The Kwapongs make a bug jungle, Chris Howarth makes sand art waves, and Rob Biddulph shows how to draw a furry monster!

programme.title: Art Ninja
programme.desc: Ricky has never been good at football, so is horrified to hear that his dad has entered him and his make-believe girlfriend into a family tournament. Ricky makes a flickball game and a dotty football boot inspired by artist Yayoi Kusama, before persuading a reluctant Gavin to don a wig and pass as his sweetheart. But how will Ricky turn a thousand balls he's accidentally ordered online into some striking art on the pitch?

programme.title: Hey You What If?
programme.desc: Find out why stopping the Earth from spinning isn’t such a good idea.

programme.title: Planet Defenders
programme.desc: Ash is passionate about protecting India’s only ape: the hoolock gibbon. Spending most of their lives in the canopy and rarely coming to ground means their populations are hugely threatened by deforestation, with their population decreasing by 90 per cent in just 30 years. Four years ago, Ashwika travelled to Assam and met a gibbon that touched her heart, and from that moment on she has wanted to protect them. On this mission she is going to a village where the people live peacefully alongside the gibbons and have done for many generations, and learns about why they care about them so much. Along the way she witnesses habitat loss with her own ‘eye in the sky’. And she meets one of India’s top gibbon scientists, who is doing everything he can to save the species with the help of local villagers.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: In Ouchology, the doctors end up in a ball-throwing fight with their kid quizmasters, and it all ends with a snot, snot, snotting! Dr Xand rolls out the crushing machine in the lab to do an experiment to find out how strong your teeth are. It’s a packed Ouch and About, with George wanting to know why we burp, Clark asking why we need to sleep, and Ana asking why people have different eye colours. We fly across New South Wales, Australia, with Jesse and the Flying Doctors, to attend his hospital appointment in Sydney. In the emergency department, Nathan has an injured arm after jumping off the slide at Edinburgh Zoo and accidentally landed it, and Isabella is there after bending her finger backwards when catching the ball in Gaelic football practice.

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: Picture Perfect is the story of a young Nigerian artist, 12-Year-old Kareem Waris who is working to put on his first exhibition in his hometown of Lagos. Waris became an international star overnight when he was asked to draw a picture of the French president Macron on a visit to Lagos. And since then has lived for his art. But his mission is not going to be easy. Following the advice from the famous Nigerian artist Arinze Stanley, this young artist decides to capture the many different sides to Lagos and travels to familiar and unknown locations in his home state to find inspiration for each drawing that will eventually find its way to the wall of his exhibition. At the nearby bustling market Waris captures the likeness of a yam seller, and we see how this attentive young boy interprets the vibrant world around him with his pencil. He heads further afield to places in Lagos he has never seen before – rich with visual inspiration, a floating fishing village and the luscious greenery and wildlife of a rural conservation centre. But will he manage to finish all these pictures in time for his first ever exhibition?

programme.title: Stage Stars
programme.desc: There is a buzz around the school as the Anything Goes cast list is finally revealed. Will Lucas get the part of Moonface, a role usually reserved for the older years? Best friends Uma and Anna are both hoping for the lead, but will they be happy with their parts? Meanwhile, singer Max has been busy writing new songs over the summer, and his dreams of life as a pop star are ever closer as he releases new music.

programme.title: The Football Academy
programme.desc: Best mates Riley and Maddie battle a boys' team, and pals Masai and Ernest swap the pitch for the kitchen. Michael Dawson is in Skill Showdown.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: The Piris take refuge in grumpy Millie the Squid’s cave during a storm but accidentally damage a painting. Can they find a clever way to fix it before Millie finds out?

programme.title: Duck and Frog

programme.title: Art Ninja
programme.desc: Ricky thinks he has nothing to spread on his toast, until a giant egg is mysteriously delivered. Worried it could contain a baby T rex, Ricky distracts himself with art, including a prehistoric mask and a paperweight inspired by Rachel Whiteread. At the park he uses his leftover toast to make a loaf-ly picture, but what will happen to the massive egg?

programme.title: Hey You What If?
programme.desc: Things don’t quite go to plan when the moon turns into cheese.

programme.title: Planet Defenders
programme.desc: Erin Ranney is a wildlife camera operator and adventurer. She splits her time between Alaska and the Falklands, and loves filming the planet’s most incredible wildlife. Erin’s mission is to film rockhopper penguins on the Falkland Islands and find out why these charismatic creatures are facing serious threats to their survival. She investigates why the rockhopper population has fallen by 80 per cent since the 1930s. Climate change is the biggest threat to the rockhoppers, causing violent storms that could see them face extinction. Erin also finds out how planting tussac grass can combat climate change and discovers that what we do in our own countries can affect these penguins thousands of miles away. She talks about how we can all make a difference towards helping the planet, just by taking small steps.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: Gladiator Dynamite is back to make sure things don’t get too explosive when Dr Dan and Dr Raph get into a Body Battle in a planktastic challenge to test their core muscles. On the basketball court, we meet Joel Henry, our Brilliant Bodies slam-dunk champion, to find out how he jumps so much higher than Dr Chris. It’s game on in the lab when Dr Xand becomes Saliva Man and Dr Chris tries everything he knows to overcome his superpowers as the doctors show us what spit is for. In an amazing Bodytricks, six kids surprise themselves by lifting Dr Ronx off a chair using only two fingers! And in the emergency department in Edinburgh, we follow the treatment of Niamh, who has broken her elbow doing a back handspring at gymnastics, and Adhya, who took a whack on the thumb during a hockey match. Ouch!

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: Sandro is a 14-year-old boy with a driving ambition to take part in the Le Mans 24-hour race. His big dream may be a few years off, but he is determined to make it happen. Sandro is a keen kart racer when we meet him, and we follow him as he pursues his dream to graduate to racing cars on professional circuits. We see him in kart-racing action, and are with him as he learns the enormity of what lies ahead if he is to achieve his dream of becoming a Formula One racing driver.
